title: ‘Stop Making Sense’ and the Top 5 Concert Films
format: Stop Making Sense returns to theaters in a 4K rerelease, and Sean, Amanda, and guest Rob Harvilla argue over the best concert film ever made before drafting their own top fives.

segments.heading: Stop Making Sense at 40
segments.note: Jonathan Demme's 1984 Talking Heads concert film, rereleased in 4K by A24

films.title: Stop Making Sense
films.note: Jonathan Demme's document of Talking Heads' 1983 tour, staged and shot as a fully constructed piece rather than a captured moment. Rereleased in 4K by A24 for its 40th anniversary. Both hosts call it the best concert film ever made, ahead of The Last Waltz.

segments.heading: Sean Fennessey's top five concert films
segments.subject: Sean Fennessey

films.title: Gimme Shelter
films.note: Albert and David Maysles and Charlotte Zwerin's document of the Rolling Stones' 1969 tour, ending at the Altamont Speedway show where a concertgoer was stabbed to death by a Hells Angel working security. Shot in the Maysles' direct-cinema style, including footage of Mick Jagger watching the violence back on an editing deck.

films.title: Amazing Grace
films.note: Sydney Pollack filmed Aretha Franklin recording her 1972 gospel album live at a small church, but the footage sat unsynced and unreleased for over 30 years until Alan Elliott finished it. Franklin blocked its release in her lifetime; it came out a year after she died.

films.title: Dave Chappelle's Block Party
films.note: Michel Gondry's film of the block party Dave Chappelle threw in Brooklyn in 2004, mixing performances from Kanye West, Erykah Badu, Mos Def, and a Fugees reunion with Chappelle putting the whole thing together.

films.title: Jazz on a Summer's Day
films.note: Bert Stern's only film as director, shot at the 1958 Newport Jazz Festival and intercut with footage of that year's America's Cup sailing races. Louis Armstrong, Thelonious Monk, and Mahalia Jackson all perform.

films.title: Shut Up and Play the Hits
films.note: Chronicles LCD Soundsystem's announced-in-advance final show at Madison Square Garden on April 2, 2011.

segments.heading: Rob Harvilla's top five concert films
segments.subject: Rob Harvilla

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: Martin Scorsese's film of The Band's farewell concert on Thanksgiving 1976 at San Francisco's Winterland Ballroom, with guest turns from Bob Dylan, Joni Mitchell, Neil Young, Van Morrison, and Neil Diamond.

films.title: Homecoming
films.note: Beyoncé directed and stars in this document of her 2018 Coachella headlining set, built around a tribute to historically Black colleges and universities. A joint pick with Amanda.

films.title: Prince: Sign 'o' the Times
films.note: Prince directed this concert film himself, staged with dialogue and comic interludes between songs; Sheila E. plays drums throughout.

films.title: Instrument
films.note: Jem Cohen's document of Fugazi, split between concert footage and behind-the-scenes material shot over the band's decade together.

films.title: Woodstock
films.note: Michael Wadleigh's document of the 1969 festival; Rob watched the 1994 director's cut, which runs 224 minutes and was edited in part by Martin Scorsese and Thelma Schoonmaker.

segments.heading: Amanda Dobbins's top five concert films
segments.subject: Amanda Dobbins

films.title: The Beatles: Get Back
films.note: Peter Jackson's reedit and expansion of outtakes from the 1969 Let It Be sessions, ending with the band's impromptu rooftop concert.

films.title: Homecoming
films.note: Beyoncé's 2018 Coachella set, built around a tribute to historically Black colleges and universities. A joint pick with Rob.

films.title: David Byrne's American Utopia
films.note: Spike Lee's film of David Byrne's Broadway show, built around Talking Heads songs and staged in explicit conversation with Stop Making Sense.

films.title: Madonna: Truth or Dare
films.note: Alek Keshishian's document of Madonna's 1990 Blond Ambition tour, shot in black and white behind the scenes and color on stage.

films.title: Fade to Black
films.note: Chronicles Jay-Z's announced retirement concert at Madison Square Garden, intercut with studio footage of The Black Album sessions with Kanye West, Pharrell, Timbaland, and Rick Rubin.

referenced.group: Also part of the concert-film conversation

Summer of Soul.title: Summer of Soul (...Or, When the Revolution Could Not Be Televised)
